name: tech-priests
title: Tech Priests
author: christopher vardeman
description: Tech-Priest logistics drones, Cogitator Stations, Machine Spirit Sanctification, ritual maintenance items, scheduler spine, station pair recovery, doctrine conversations, diegetic diagnostic GUIs, strict command hierarchy, action arbitration, dispatcher-owned acquisition/production/consecration/repair/combat-repair, logistics/construction physical access, persistent portrait assignment, structured Work State tabs, diegetic Work-State Reliquary polish, Machine-Spirit ledger traits, machine-type-aware trait taxonomy, expanded priest dossiers, universal known-resource fetch, and 0.1.528 dispatcher-owned machine logistics fulfillment for non-automated assemblers/furnaces: clear outputs, sort detritus, and physically supply fuel/ingredients before raw mining or emergency crafting, and 0.1.531 operational sound pass with machine run/start/wind-down sounds, BIOS key-clatter, UI click cues, and occasional Tech-Priest respirator barks, and 0.1.532 active-writ status churn damping plus brown/green diegetic Cogitator GUI tinting, and 0.1.533 functional placeholder audio integration with manifest-preserving OGG assets, cooldown-governed event hooks, station/pair cues, machine sanctity/clog cues, and diegetic GUI feedback, and 0.1.534 graphical asset integration for station-specific Cogitator art, Martian emergency machine sprites, enlarged/filter-enforced stone caches, basic/advanced space cargo inventories, and resized primitive void steam machinery, and 0.1.536 mechanically sliced Cogitator GUI frame integration for the Work-State Reliquary using fixed corners/emblems, stretch-safe rails/columns, and a 9-slice inner aperture around existing native GUI content. 0.1.539 fixes direct-acquisition movement/work visual split-brain by suppressing mining beams while walking, clearing stale movement leases when work begins, and preserving adjacent-only mining visuals. 0.1.541 applies the next visual/live-test correction pass: tiled GUI rails/columns, stronger terminal wrapping/tab preservation, Martian emergency machine visual rescaling and sound balancing, station icon normalization, Planetary Magos station vertical offset repair, doubled void cargo inventory footprint, and a stronger direct-acquisition work clamp to stop walking while mining. 0.1.542 performs a mod-wide localization coverage pass for visible prototype families, adds retained equipment-grid/projectile descriptions, normalizes technology order/prerequisites for a cleaner recipe-aware chain, and adds consecration target claims so multiple priests do not all attempt the same machine-spirit rite. 0.1.543 retargets Blackstone asteroid bodies, asteroid chunks, and cloned particle debris to the included Blackstone image assets instead of inheriting vanilla metallic/iron asteroid sprite sheets. 0.1.544 adds explicit mechanically sliced Blackstone particle prototype families to fix missing particle assignID, lowers the Planetary Magos Cogitator Station sprite further, and repairs sanctified mining drill operation counting with products-finished/output/progress sensors. 0.1.547 audits the pressurized fluid vault storage-tank clone path and strips inherited vanilla fluid/window/pipe-cover overlays so the custom stone vault no longer renders a stray gray nub below the sprite. 0.1.550 fixes the pressurized fluid vault pipe positions so Transport Drones bounding-box adjustments cannot push north/south fluid connections outside the storage-tank entity bounds. 0.1.551 corrects the primitive acclimator battery bank to use Factorio 2.0 chargable_graphics with the custom stone-cache battery-bank artwork, and restores pressurized fluid vault pipe access with valid final-stage north/south pipe-grid connections after Transport Drones compatibility edits. 0.1.555 adds a registry-owned hidden proxy turret heartbeat that reattaches any detached proxy to its visible Tech-Priest shell, adopts a nearby unowned proxy when safe, or recreates the proxy if necessary without creating a new combat or movement authority. 0.1.560 replaces the portrait source sheets with the trimmed portrait archive, regenerates normalized per-cell portrait sprites from first-principles sheet dimensions, and limits Planetary Magos portraits to the valid 9x7 trimmed cells so the lost bottom row is never referenced. 0.1.561 adds sanctioned order-history authority ledgers for every Tech-Priest rank and extends the Conclave Center with deterministic map-seeded doctrine/technology alignment, authority display, and loyalty pressure from excessive emergency construction. 0.1.566 adds a movement enforcement governor that rejects stale far movement requests, clears hidden resource/combat anchors outside a station work envelope, and returns overleashed priests home through the existing movement authority to stop long-distance wandering without adding a new task selector. 0.1.568 performs an economy-efficiency governor pass over diagnostics, heartbeat/order-refresh logging, resource-expansion scanning, and selected nth-tick services so large priest deployments stagger their work instead of producing synchronized task churn. 0.1.572 adds an unobserved transit economy governor so offscreen, in-radius Tech-Priest work travel can teleport instead of issuing expensive pathing commands, while observed priests still walk normally and executors still own actual work completion. 0.1.584 adds an obstacle slap-fight guard that detects movement-owned Tech-Priests attacking neutral rocks/trees while pathing, stops the futile melee loop, and routes the obstruction through a budgeted clearing pulse so blocked movement does not become hours of vestigial unit punching. 0.1.586 adds optional lean GUI sprite mode, generating half-resolution decorative GUI SpritePrototype copies behind a startup setting while preserving full-resolution defaults. 0.1.589 adds Conclave schism governance settings for rebellion enablement and optional defecting-doctrine machine seizure, resets loyal doctrine-family loyalty after a schism so newly placed priests remain loyal, and surfaces schism policy/wave history in the Doctrine Web. 0.1.591 turns Data Spike reclamation into a tech-gated timed capture system with four reclamation tiers, repeatable defensive hardening, player-only spike use, visible cancellable countdowns, and hard-loyal Void Doctrine schism immunity. 0.1.592 makes doctrine schisms surface-scoped to the majority surface of the defecting doctrine, adds stocked-station emergency stand-down timers, and records recent loyalty causes in the Conclave and Work-State doctrine relation webs. 0.1.596 adds an early passive-service austerity gate that wraps legacy direct nth-tick handlers before they register, so new games do not run passive Tech-Priest services until an actual Tech-Priest runtime entity exists. 0.1.604 audits existing economy governors, preserves 0579 as the canonical indexed dirty/cache authority, routes repair work discovery through the existing indexed catalog before direct scans, and expands /tp-runtime-report with a live efficiency authority inventory. 0.1.605 consolidates timing authority by migrating five recurring direct nth-tick services into the runtime broker and expanding /tp-runtime-report with registry route, broker service, and fallback audit counts. 0.1.606 refines runtime telemetry with split broker skip counters, rolling-window metrics, unified scan accounting, movement/pathing counters, and future efficiency candidate documentation without adding a new runtime authority. 0.1.607 begins the Future Efficiency Candidates implementation with an event-driven work feeder: damage events submit repair work into the existing shared work queue, report event-fed submissions, and avoid adding any new scheduler/cache/sleep authority. 0.1.608 adds directed repair wakeups to that feeder: damage events wake the nearest relevant pair through existing bucket/sleep authorities, clear stale negative knowledge through the existing dirty-aware helper where applicable, and expand runtime telemetry without adding another scheduler/cache/sleep authority. 0.1.610 adds a cache-first scan-routing helper for repeated discovery paths, routing repair, consecration, pickup, retention-container, and resource doctrine scans through the existing indexed catalog before direct surface scans while preserving direct-scan fallbacks and telemetry. 0.1.611 reduces movement-controller polling by servicing active movement requests through the runtime broker instead of scanning every pair each movement tick. 0.1.612 continues scan-routing and polling reduction by routing construction placement/resource scans, logistics fetch loose-item scans, and machine-fulfillment automation/machine/container scans through the existing cache-first scan router instead of adding a new cache or scheduler authority. 0.1.613 reduces event/queue pressure by refreshing folded duplicate work orders, tracking claim scan pressure, and extending the event feeder to invalidate existing dirty/negative knowledge on build/mine/destroy events without creating a new cache, scheduler, or task authority. 0.1.614 adds an internal spatial claim index inside the existing work queue authority so nearby pairs can claim nearby queued work before falling back to full category scans, reducing queue claim pressure without adding another queue authority. 0.1.617 adds distributed subordinate assignment doctrine so eligible same-rank superior command chains share nearby lower-rank subordinates by load before proximity, and emergency cascade consumes the canonical hierarchy slate before broad fallback discovery. 0.1.618 adds adaptive runtime broker budget weighting inside the existing broker authority so active categories can receive temporary budget boosts based on rolling telemetry without adding another scheduler or timing layer. 0.1.619 caps full work-queue fallback claim scans beneath the existing work queue authority so large backlogs defer instead of causing one-tick scan spikes. 0.1.620 rotates work-queue and reservation cleanup by category beneath existing authorities to reduce maintenance spike pressure. 0.1.621 continues movement command funnel adoption by routing selected direct-acquisition, emergency-production, overleash, and recovery fallback go-to commands through the existing movement controller before raw engine set_command fallbacks, reducing duplicate path requests without adding another movement authority. 0.1.622 adds a diegetic Conclave Task Auspex / Debug Readout tab and /tp-task-auspex command that surface existing runtime, queue, reservation, sleep/wake, scan/path, movement, and selected-pair order-stack telemetry in game without adding any new runtime authority. 0.1.623 makes that Auspex cheap enough to leave open by adding compact overview rendering, refresh-throttle protection, and lazy section rendering so the debug UI does not become its own UPS tax. 0.1.624 reduces command-hierarchy rebuild pressure by adding topology-signature skips so distributed subordinate slates rebuild only when station/rank topology changes, and surfaces the saved rebuilds in runtime diagnostics.
